results.title: Otters snap four game skid with win over ThunderBolts
results.description: EVANSVILLE, Ind. (WEHT) — The Evansville Otters even up the series with the Windy City ThunderBolts with a 5-0 victory in game two. The Otters can claim the series on July 19 with first pitch set for 5:05 p.m. at Bosse Field.

results.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates as Seventh Night of Strikes Follows Ceasefire Collapse
results.description: US forces carried out a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iranian military targets after President Donald Trump declared the temporary ceasefire “over,” while Iran responded with attacks on US allies including Kuwait. The renewed fighting has killed two US service members, damaged infrastructure in Kuwait, and prompted missile interceptions by Jordan and Bahrain.

results.title: Tulsi Gabbard’s brother jailed for ‘trying to lure children’ to hotel room: report
results.description: Batarti Gabbard, the 55-year-old brother of former Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, was arrested Friday for allegedly trying to lure children to a hotel room in Honolulu, Hawaii using “gum and money,” the Honolulu Star-Advertiser reported Saturday. “Batarti Gabbard, aka Bhakti Gabbard, allegedly approached several children, including a boy, age 9, and offered them gum and money to accompany him to his hotel room, according to police,” the outlet reported. “He also asked the children for their names and wrote them in a notebook, police said. The children refused to go with him and the suspect walked away.” Batarti Gabbard's wife, Kimberly Gabbard, petitioned for a protective order from her husband on Friday due to what she described as “bizarre behavior” he exhibited the day prior, which included “singing loudly” and “disrupting others” in public, the report reads. “His family members and I have repeatedly encouraged him to seek psychiatric treatment due to his significant deterioration of his mental state and judgment,” Kimberly Gabbard wrote in her petition, according to the Honolulu Star-Advertiser. Tulsi Gabbard began her career in politics as a Hawaii state House representative in 2002, climbing the ranks over decades before being appointed as Director of National Intelligence last year, a position she resigned from last month citing her husband’s deteriorating health. Recent reporting revealed that much of Tulsi Gabbard’s decision making – from what she said to what bill she supported – may have been heavily influenced by Chris Butler, the leader of a group countless ex-members have described as a “cult.” Batarti Gabbard was charged with second-degree custodial interference , a misdemeanor.

results.title: UN Ambassador Bursts Mamdani's Bubble Over His Wish to Arrest Israeli PM Netanyahu in NYC
results.description: When will the members of the far-Left cabal learn? New York City, as the home of the United Nations headquarters, is hosting the U.N. General Assembly in the fall. And Democratic Socialist NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani expressed during a recent interview with a New York Times interviewer that he's holding onto a deeply-held wish and campaign promise: to have Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu arrested when the world leader arrives in his city to attend it.“I believe that Prime Minister Netanyahu belongs in the Hague,” Mr. Mamdani told Lulu Garcia-Navarro this week on “The Interview,” a New York Times show,...

results.description: The National Weather Service in San Angelo has issued a Flood Advisory for West El Paso, Santa Teresa, and Sunland Park until 9:45 PM. results.title: India News | UP: Jauhar University Given Extra Week to Submit Fire Safety Documents as Several Buildings Flagged for Non-compliance
results.description: Get latest articles and stories on India at LatestLY. A Fire Department team formed by a committee constituted to access fire safety at education institutions and commercial buildings have flagged non-compliance at Mohammad Ali Jauhar University, the flagship project of jailed senior Samajwadi Party leader Mohammad Azam Khan, with officials saying that of the roughly 38 to 40 buildings on the premises, many lack functional firefighting equipment.
